query_normalized: evaluating the robustness of llm-generated code to input perturbations computer science
rationale: This paper qualifies under category (d) as a foundational methodology and canonical reference for the concept of "adversarial robustness" and "input perturbations" in deep learning, which are the core mechanisms the user is investigating, even though the paper is a general survey rather than a specific study on LLM-generated code. It establishes the theoretical framework and vocabulary (attacks, defenses, verification) necessary to define the robustness evaluation the user proposes.
